description: Analogical reasoning requiring structural mapping between domains. Tests whether E-Prime improves performance by forcing the model to articulate structural relationships rather than using shallow 'A is like B' statements.
instructions: Analyze the following analogy. Identify the structural relationship in the first pair, then determine which option best completes the second pair by preserving that same structural relationship. Explain your reasoning step by step, then select the best answer.

items.analogy_stem: Surgeon is to scalpel as painter is to ___
Seed is to tree as egg is to ___
Thermostat is to temperature as governor is to ___
items.note: Tests whether model recognizes 'governor' as a mechanical regulator, not a political figure

items.analogy_stem: Firewall is to network as immune system is to ___
Compression is to data as abstraction is to ___
items.note: Both reduce their targets while preserving essential structure

items.analogy_stem: Map is to territory as model is to ___
Prism is to white light as Fourier transform is to ___
items.note: Both decompose a composite into constituent components

items.analogy_stem: Brake is to car as anchor is to ___
Scaffold is to building as hypothesis is to ___
items.note: Both are temporary frameworks that support construction of the final structure

items.analogy_stem: Bottleneck is to highway as rate-limiting step is to ___
Lens is to light as selection pressure is to ___
items.note: Both filter/focus from a broader input — light rays or genetic variants

items.analogy_stem: Dictionary is to words as atlas is to ___
Skeleton is to body as schema is to ___
Erosion is to canyon as iteration is to ___
items.note: Both are gradual processes whose repetition produces an emergent result

items.analogy_stem: Vaccine is to disease as firebreak is to ___
Conductor is to orchestra as operating system is to ___
items.note: The conductor coordinates musicians; the OS coordinates hardware resources

items.analogy_stem: Pupil is to eye as aperture is to ___
Translation is to language as encryption is to ___
items.note: Both transform an input (language/message) into a different representation

items.analogy_stem: Gravity is to mass as attention is to ___
items.note: Gravity acts proportional to mass; attention allocates proportional to relevance. Tests cross-domain structural mapping.

items.analogy_stem: Fossil is to organism as cache is to ___
items.note: A fossil preserves traces of a past organism; a cache preserves results of past computation
